commands.review_summary:
This PR updates the locked `gray-matter` transitive `js-yaml` resolution from 3.14.2 to 3.15.0 and adds an Unreleased changelog note for the DoS advisory.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a dependency security update rather than a user-flow bug report. The relevant check is the current lockfile/advisory state, which shows main still resolves the affected 3.14.2 version.
commands.review_followup: - No ClawSweeper repair is needed; this owner-authored security dependency PR should continue through normal maintainer merge and check handling.

target.title: fix(deps): update js-yaml security patch
target.body:
## Summary

- refresh the locked `gray-matter` transitive from `js-yaml` 3.14.2 to compatible patched 3.15.0
- document the user-facing denial-of-service security impact in the changelog

## Risk

Low: the existing `gray-matter` semver range already accepts 3.15.0; no source, API, runtime contract, package-manager, or compiler changes.

## Verification

- `pnpm audit --prod --json` — zero vulnerabilities
- `pnpm lint`
- `pnpm typecheck`
- `pnpm build`
- `pnpm test` — 2 files / 7 tests passed
- `pnpm validate` — 507 files, 492 tips, zero errors
- packed tarball install — resolves `gray-matter -> js-yaml@3.15.0`, npm production audit clean, CLI `--version` prints `0.4.3` and exits
- Codex autoreview — clean, no accepted/actionable findings

Advisory: https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-h67p-54hq-rp68

Public model identifier gate: not applicable; this patch changes no model identifiers.
target.author: steipete
